title: lavandula stoechas var. pseudostoechas
intro: Lavandula stoechas var. pseudostoechas, also known as Dyer's Lavender or False French Lavender, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region and North Africa. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory abilities, derived from its essential oil rich in camphor and borneol. The health benefits of Lavandula stoechas var. pseudostoechas include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate respiratory issues such as colds and bronchitis. Lavandula stoechas var. pseudostoechas can be used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, as well as in aromatherapy and skin care products to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
